title: Accenture plc (ACN) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of Accenture plc (ACN), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: Accenture plc receives a score of 67/100, reflecting its current competitive position and forward outlook. The company demonstrates solid fundamentals with some areas of concern that moderate the overall assessment.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Accenture plc shows moderate competitive momentum.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Accenture plc's revenue growth is above industry peers.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Market share has been stable in key segments.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The company demonstrates strong pricing power in its markets.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Product innovation and launch cadence is above average.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Accenture plc's competitive moat is moderate.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Customer switching costs are high due to integration depth.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: IP portfolio and regulatory position provide strong competitive protection.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Scale advantages create limited barriers to entry.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment toward Accenture plc is positive.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analyst estimates have been revised upward.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Media and analyst narrative is positive.

keyRisks: AI automation reducing demand for human consultants long-term
Cyclical enterprise IT spending in recession
Wage inflation in key talent markets
keyCatalysts: Enterprise GenAI spending accelerating from experimentation to production-scale deployment could drive Accenture's AI bookings from $3B to $10B+ annually by 2028, creating a new growth engine
Managed services expansion as companies outsource increasingly complex IT operations to Accenture creates recurring revenue streams with higher margins than project-based consulting
Government digital transformation mandates globally (US federal modernization, EU digital services) provide a $50B+ addressable market with Accenture's security clearances as a competitive barrier
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored ACN at 67/100 and Opus at 78/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
